narrative.summary: Swedish-inspired in turquoise bay-and-gable; owner (2017 TO AeroPress champ) now roasting in-house as Lycka Coffee Roasters.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You'll spot the turquoise bay-and-gable Victorian from a block away on one of Kensington's quieter residential edges. Inside, natural light floods through tall windows onto blonde wood tables, and there's that rare thing—a back patio that actually feels removed from the chaos. The front fills with laptop people; the back patio is where you can hear yourself think.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is proper Swedish fika: coffee and something sweet to break up your day, not a full meal service. The owner won Toronto's AeroPress championship in 2017 and now roasts in-house under the name Lycka Coffee Roasters, so the espresso pulls are dialed in. You'll find a walnut-hazelnut brownie that reviewers loop back for, seasonal drinks like strawberry matcha, and fika iced lattes that balance milk and acidity without getting syrupy. They do have savoury options, but the draw here is the coffee program.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Mid-morning on a weekday when you need to reset between errands, or a slow weekend afternoon on the back patio with a book. This isn't a workspace or a hangout spot—it's for the deliberate pause the Swedes built into their day.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐ "The very definition of “cute”!
If you’re looking for a peaceful spot near Kensington, this place is a gem. The natural lighting is lovely, warm, and inviting. I tried their fika iced latte and the walnut-hazelnut brownie — both were delicious. They also offer savoury food options. There’s both a front and back patio, perfect for enjoying a quiet moment."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I had their Strawberry Matcha drink back in the summer and it was great. There’s also a beautiful and spacious patio, which is perfect on a sunny day.

A minor downside for me was that they didn’t take Amex, but nothing to take away from the overall experience."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Stellar Scandinavian coffee shop that is a specialty/craft/third wave coffee shop. It hits the trifecta. 1. Great coffee- their house roast is good but also is a multi roaster and had several high end roasters available. Pretty rare these days. 2. Great aesthetics and comfortable. This place has out front and outdoor back patio seating as well as indoor dining. 3. Great place to hang out with friends and catch up. Definitely good for that. Best coffee in Toronto!!"
